title: Rate Limiting API Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the rate limiting api capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The rate-limit lifecycle is explicit: an operator sets a per-tenant policy, evaluation checks entitlement before counting, requests move within a fixed window from allowed below or at the limit to denied above the limit, and live counts reset in a new window bucket. No configured policy means opt-in allow.

stateModel.description: Policy state consists of policy key, entitlement key, limit, window seconds, action, and updated-at/updated-by; counter state is per tenant, policy, and window with a used count and an exceeded state once the used count passes the limit. Counters are bucketed by window and reset on a new bucket rather than being decremented.

permissions.description: Tenant developer reads use a tenant developer-read permission, operator policy read/write use platform rate-limit permissions, and policy evaluation additionally requires the configured entitlement. Operator routes validate the tenant identifier and policy mutation is operator-only.

contracts.description: API contracts are a tenant rate-limit read, an operator per-tenant rate-limit read, and an operator per-tenant rate-limit update, with developer-portal exposure of rate-limit summaries. The interface is a rate-limit port with both a cache adapter and a relational adapter behind it.

validation.description: Validation proves that no policy is opt-in allow, a missing entitlement denies before counting, configured limits allow below/at the limit and deny above it, and an invalid tenant identifier fails the operator operation. Entitlement is evaluated before the counter is consulted.

errorModel.description: An assert operation throws once the limit is exceeded while an evaluate operation returns denied decisions distinguishing entitlement denial from exceeded state; when the cache provider is unavailable it reports degraded and falls back to relational counting. Decisions fail closed on ambiguous provider state.

auditModel.description: Setting a rate-limit policy emits an audit record before the mutation (audit-before-change) with the rate-limit resource identified, so policy changes are attributable. Evaluation decisions are observable for enforcement.

readinessModel.description: The relational proof skips honestly when the database is unavailable; the cache provider reports ready on a successful ping and degraded on failure, with relational fallback still valid for counting. Readiness never claims availability beyond what the substrate proves.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by rate-limit unit tests and runtime proofs validating live relational and cache counting plus operator route behaviour and degraded fallback. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
